narration: ProofPay proves what an autonomous agent paid for. This prerecorded evidence walkthrough documents a real successful Hedera testnet x402 payment completed on July twenty-first, twenty twenty-six. No new payment executes during playback.

A blockchain transaction proves that value moved between accounts. But it does not prove what an API returned. ProofPay closes that gap by binding the payment to the request, evidence, output, provenance, and software version.

Here is the complete flow. Codex requests evidence. The resource server returns HTTP four oh two. Codex signs the exact HBAR transfer. Blocky four oh two verifies and sponsors the network fee. Hedera settles, the evidence unlocks, and ProofPay issues a signed receipt.

This slide reconstructs the completed purchase from the committed proof bundle. Value buyer account zero point zero point nine six seven six zero seven four authorized one million one tinybar to seller account zero point zero point nine six seven six zero seven three. Facilitator account zero point zero point seven one six two seven eight four paid the network fee. No signing key is shown.

Hedera's sponsored fee model separates authorization from network fees. The buyer signs the exact value transfer. Blocky four oh two validates it, adds the fee payer signature, and submits it. ProofPay releases nothing until settlement succeeds.

For the performed transaction, ProofPay mirror-verified the buyer debit, seller credit, exact amount, network, consensus result, and transaction identifier. It then unlocked deterministic DeltaSignal MSTR filing evidence and produced the committed Ed25519 signed receipt binding payment, delivery, and provenance.

This is the real Hedera testnet transaction we completed: zero point zero point seven one six two seven eight four, dash, one seven eight four six six five one nine two, dash, nine zero six nine eight nine five nine five. HashScan and the Hedera mirror node independently show success, value buyer, seller, fee payer, amount, and consensus timestamp.

HashScan proves money moved. The portable verifier proves what the money unlocked. Sixteen checks validate the trusted signer, quote, payment terms, request hash, encrypted payload, decrypted artifact, evidence hash, source date, output hash, and settlement claims.

ProofPay fails closed. Change a single field in the delivered evidence and verification rejects the bundle. The signed artifact and evidence hashes no longer match, so mutation is detected immediately.

For DeltaSignal, this creates pay-per-evidence infrastructure for autonomous agents. Buyers pay only for the research they need. This demonstrated delivery is auditable, portable across payment rails, and backed by public settlement proof.
Thank you for watching. The repository includes the performed payment bundle, the public transaction link, this slide-by-slide transcript, and the verifier needed to reproduce our evidence checks.
